task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles an empty string as balanced.
trace: The function takes a string `s` as input and returns `True` if the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')'. It uses a stack to keep track of opening parentheses. For every opening parenthesis, it is pushed onto the stack. For every closing parenthesis, it checks whether there's a corresponding opening parenthesis on top of the stack or if the stack is empty or if the stack's top element is not an opening parenthesis. If any of these conditions are met, it returns `False`. The function returns `True` only if all closing parentheses have corresponding opening parentheses and no closing parenthesis appears before its opening partner. An edge case handled here is when there's an empty string as input, which is considered balanced.
